summary: Matched two ftCommon Attack100 air item-scope enter functions, `fn_800D7C60` and `fn_800D81D0`, by adding C implementations in `ftCo_Attack100.c`. The PR body identifies them as the air scope rapid and air scope fire enter variants, modeled after ground counterparts and calling `ftCommon_ClampAirDrift` after `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`.

key_files.role: Added matched C bodies for `fn_800D7C60` and `fn_800D81D0`.

what_changed: `fn_800D7C60(Fighter_GObj* gobj)` was implemented as the air scope rapid enter function, using `fn_800D769C(fp, ftCo_MS_ItemScopeAirRapid)`, `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`, `ftCommon_ClampAirDrift`, `p_ftCommonData + 0x5BC`, `fn_800D80F4`, and `fn_800D8378`.

`fn_800D81D0(Fighter_GObj* gobj, int arg1)` was implemented as the air scope fire enter function, clearing `fp->throw_flags`, using `fn_800D769C(fp, ftCo_MS_ItemScopeAirFire)`, `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`, `ftCommon_ClampAirDrift`, assigning `fp->mv.co.common.x0 = arg1`, and installing `fn_800D86B8` plus `fn_800D8378` callbacks.
Both new functions were placed near their related ground counterparts and wrapped with local `#pragma dont_inline` regions.
The decomp.dev report showed `fn_800D81D0` gaining 152 matched bytes and `fn_800D7C60` gaining 140 matched bytes, both moving from 0.00% to 100.00%.
smart_moves: Used the already-matched ground variants as templates: PR text explicitly calls `fn_800D7C60` the air version of `fn_800D7BDC` and `fn_800D81D0` the air version of `fn_800D8140`.
Preserved the important air-specific behavior by calling `ftCommon_ClampAirDrift(fp)` immediately after `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` in both functions.
Used codegen-shaping locals such as `Fighter_GObj* temp_gobj`, a scoped `HSD_GObj* gobj2`, and `PAD_STACK(8)` where needed.
Matched existing nearby style for float constants by declaring `extern int ftCo_804D9028/902C/9030/9034` and loading them through `*(f32*) &symbol` casts.
decomp_lessons: For fighter ground/air motion-state pairs, cloning the structure and assignment order from the ground counterpart can be an effective matching strategy, with the air variant adding `ftCommon_ClampAirDrift` after the motion-state transition.
The order of side effects matters: compute motion ID, call `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`, clamp air drift, then write `mv.co.common.x0` and callback fields.
Seemingly redundant temporaries, scoped casts, and stack padding are valid matching tools when the target assembly requires particular register allocation or stack shape.
When semantic names are not established, keeping address-based function names is preferable to inventing labels.
naming_conventions: Common fighter motion IDs use the `ftCo_MS_` prefix, shown here by `ftCo_MS_ItemScopeAirRapid` and `ftCo_MS_ItemScopeAirFire`.
Common fighter state access follows the existing `fp->mv.co.common.x0` field path.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `#pragma push`, `#pragma dont_inline on`, and `#pragma pop` around the newly matched functions.
Used raw float loads from unresolved data symbols via `*(f32*) &ftCo_804D9028`, `*(f32*) &ftCo_804D902C`, `*(f32*) &ftCo_804D9030`, and `*(f32*) &ftCo_804D9034`.
Used a raw common-data offset load: `*(s32*) ((u8*) p_ftCommonData + 0x5BC)`.
Introduced gobj temporaries (`temp_gobj`, scoped `HSD_GObj* gobj2`) to match call codegen for `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`.
Used exact motion flags from the diff: `0x02000000` for the rapid air enter and `Ft_MF_None` for the fire air enter.
review_feedback: No human review comments or formal reviews were present in the slice.
decomp.dev bot reported the two target functions as new 100.00% matches: `fn_800D81D0` +152 bytes and `fn_800D7C60` +140 bytes.
follow_up_queries: Search nearby ftCommon Attack100 functions for more ground/air pairs where a matched ground variant can guide an air variant.
Investigate the semantic field name for `p_ftCommonData + 0x5BC` when ftCommonData struct coverage improves.
Look for future PRs that rename or document `fn_800D7C60` and `fn_800D81D0` with semantic item-scope enter names.
Check whether `ftCo_804D9028`, `ftCo_804D902C`, `ftCo_804D9030`, and `ftCo_804D9034` later gain typed float declarations without disrupting matches.
